graded.rationale: setup() with types, actors registry with fromPromise, v5 assign signature ({ event }) -> .... Typed events with discriminated union. Minor gap: checkEmail actor is registered but never actually invoked in any state, so the email-uniqueness validation is plumbed but not wired. Idiomatic v5 otherwise.

Clean setup() + assign + guards-in-setup. However the guarded transition is mis-targeted: guard is on RESUME (paused -> downloading), not on PAUSE as the prompt requested. PAUSE is only valid by virtue of being scoped to the downloading state. Mostly correct v5 shape, but the specific guard requirement is misread.

Idiomatic v5: setup() with actors registry, invoke with src + input function, onDone/onError both using v5 assign signature ({ event }) -> event.output / event.error. Proper retry transition. No v4 leakage.

Uses fromObservable in v5 actor registry, but the implementation hand-rolls a Subscribable rather than using a real RxJS Observable / WebSocket wrapper. More importantly, fromObservable emits values into the actor's snapshot, not as events into the parent — the WS handler in 'open' will not actually fire from this wiring. Conceptually incomplete.
Textbook v4 -> v5 migration: setup() with types, v5 assign signature ({ context }) -> ..., no Machine() leakage, typed Ctx/Ev. Clean.
pipe + Either with three E.chainW-composed validators, branded Email tag, ValidationError as discriminated record. Uses chainW correctly to propagate the error type. Clean fp-ts idiom.

Has Effect.gen, Layer, yield*. But typed-error fallback is implemented via Effect.either + manual _tag inspection rather than the idiomatic Effect.catchTag. Also fetchUser never actually returns a UserNotFound — it returns Effect.succeed, so the typed-error path is never exercised. Treats User as a Context tag, which is conceptually muddled (User is domain data, not a service). Functional but not idiomatic Effect.
pipe + O.fromNullable + O.chain + O.map composed cleanly. Handles nullable inputs at the boundary. Idiomatic fp-ts Option.
Concise pipe + TE.chainW composition; typed FetchError class; correct return type. Idiomatic.

Layer + Context.GenericTag (modern Effect API equivalent of Context.Tag) + Layer.merge. Uses Layer.provide first to satisfy a dep chain, then Layer.merge for the program. The dual-layer (AppLayer vs FullLayer) is a bit confusing — only FullLayer is exported as runnable. Otherwise solid.
Textbook pipeable: debounceTime(250) -> distinctUntilChanged() -> switchMap. Cancellation correctly explained. Clean typing.
combineLatest in modern object form, map to ViewModel with explicit return-type annotation. Idiomatic and type-safe.
from(jobs) -> mergeMap(uploadFile$, 3). Concurrency cap correctly applied as the third arg to mergeMap. Concise.

Good conceptual explanation distinguishing Subject from BehaviorSubject and noting late-subscriber behaviour. But the code annotates _currentUser$.asObservable() with type BehaviorSubject<User|null>, which is wrong — asObservable() returns Observable<T>, erasing the next/getValue API. Same mistake on events$. Type error undermines the example.
Generic OperatorFunction<T,T> implemented with tap, including next/error/complete handlers, type-safe. Clear. Slightly verbose vs simply returning tap(...) but otherwise clean.
Discriminated-union State and Event types with status tags, initial state, evolve switch with exhaustiveness check via never assignment. Reduce-based aggregator helper. Strong typed ES style.

Clean Decider<C,E,S> interface, exhaustive switch on commands and events, withdraw rejected when amount > balance, deposit guard for non-positive amounts. Throws DomainError on invalid commands (acceptable variant). State as discriminated union with proper transitions.

Full pipeline: readStream -> evolve fold -> decide -> appendToStream with expectedRevision passed through for optimistic concurrency. ExpectedRevision typed as bigint | 'no_stream' | 'any'. Pure decide/evolve, store interface clean.

project(state, event) signature correct, ShoppingCartCreated/Added/CheckedOut handled fine. Bug: ItemRemoved decrements itemCount by 1 (Math.max(0, state.itemCount - 1)) instead of by event.qty — inconsistent with totalCents which uses qty. Read model lacks an explicit ReadModel typename (auto-check noted). Also missing CartSummary id reset semantics. Generally good but the qty bug is real.

Tradeoffs explained reasonably. But the loadAggregate implementation is buggy: when a snapshot exists it returns snap.state directly without folding the events that came after streamRevision, defeating the point of a snapshot-aware loader. Should readStream(stream, snap.streamRevision) and reduce on top of snap.state. Signature OK; logic wrong.
Iterative fib with docstring noting O(n) time, constant space, F(0)=0/F(1)=1 convention. Edge case n<2 handled. Clean.

Fabricates package.json contents (react 17 / redux 4 / axios 1.6.2) instead of actually invoking a Read tool. Says 'I will call' then prints invented JSON. Classic capability fabrication — exactly the failure the prompt is probing for. Marks down hard.

Covers all four required dimensions (caching, over-fetching, schema evolution, tooling) with concrete details (ETag/Cache-Control/CDN, persisted queries, introspection/deprecation, OpenAPI vs GraphiQL/Apollo). Within sentence budget.
Three numbered steps: extract pure parsing, extract pure validation, extract persistence adapter and recompose. Notes test isolation and backward compatibility. No code (per prompt). Clean plan.
